commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1dfb741f1a8d6e108ff65b52069529c20d7ffbfc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR derives a single `agent:<id>` Usage query token, passes it as `agentId` to `sessions.usage`, seeds Usage filter options from `agents.list`, reloads on server-side agent-scope changes, and adds focused UI/controller tests.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +129. Total +164 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue gives a concrete multi-agent flow, and current source shows the UI omits `agentId` while the gateway scopes omitted requests to the default agent; I did not run a live browser reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for the compatibility blocker and real behavior proof; this is not a safe automation-only repair because the external PR proof must demonstrate the contributor's or maintainer's real setup.

target.title: fix(ui): scope usage requests by agent filter
target.body:
Fixes #87132.

## Summary
- pass a single selected `agent:<id>` usage query through to `sessions.usage` as `agentId`
- populate the Usage page agent filter from `agents.list` so non-main agents are selectable before their usage sessions are loaded
- reload usage data when the applied query changes the server-side agent scope

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render-usage-tab.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ui/src/ui/app-render-usage-tab.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage.ts ui/src/ui/views/usageTypes.ts ui/src/ui/app-render-usage-tab.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: The Control UI Usage page rendered an agent filter but `loadUsage` always called `sessions.usage` without `agentId`, so the gateway scoped omitted requests to the default/main agent and non-main usage could not be loaded from that filter.

**Real environment tested**: Local openclaw checkout at head `1dfb741f1a8d6e108ff65b52069529c20d7ffbfc` on Linux with the repository UI controller and Usage view tests.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) node --import tsx -e 'import { resolveUsageAgentIdFromQuery } from "./ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.ts"; const cases = ["provider:openai agent:research ", "agent:research agent:main", "agent:research*"]; console.log(`head=${process.env.HEAD}`); for (const query of cases) console.log(`${query} => ${resolveUsageAgentIdFromQuery(query) ?? "<none>"}`);'`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
head=1dfb741f1a8d6e108ff65b52069529c20d7ffbfc
provider:openai agent:research  => research
agent:research agent:main => <none>
agent:research* => <none>
```

**Observed result after fix**: A single concrete `agent:research` Usage query now resolves to `research`, which `loadUsage` includes as `agentId` in `sessions.usage`; ambiguous multi-agent and globbed filters remain client-side only.

**What was not tested**: I did not run a live browser against a multi-agent gateway; validation covered the UI controller request payload, Usage tab reload behavior, and rendered agent filter options with local Vitest.
target.author: alix-007
